evidence_refs.source_quote: Update to the MTA Board December 17, 2025

tool_args.label: CityTicket sales grew 107% from 2023 to 2025
tool_args.raw_text: CityTicket sales grew 122% from 2022 to 2023 and 107% from 2023 to 2025

tool_args.label: Major felonies rate 1.51 per 1M riders in 2025 YTD
Crime rate decreased ~13% from 2024 to 2025 YTD
tool_args.raw_text: From 2024 to 2025 YTD the crime rate per 1M riders has decreased ~13%

tool_args.label: Major felonies per 1M riders has fallen ~56% over 5 years
tool_args.raw_text: Over the last five years, major felonies per 1M riders has fallen ~56%

tool_args.label: 57% felt safe on subway in January 2025
71% felt safe on subway in November 2025
Broadway-60 St ridership +18% YoY
tool_args.raw_text: Broadway-60 St N, Q, R, W +18%

tool_args.label: 11% fewer vehicles entering CBD (71,000 fewer per day)
tool_args.raw_text: 11% fewer vehicles that enter the CBD (71,000 fewer per day)

tool_args.label: Car speeds within CBD up 4.3%
tool_args.raw_text: Car speeds within the CBD are up 4.3%

tool_args.label: PM2.5 down 22% in first six months
tool_args.raw_text: Air quality improved: PM2.5 down 22% in the first six months

payload.claim_text: Includes over 90% of state-of-good-repair investments. Highlights: 2,000 new railcars, 150 subway and 25 railroad station rehabs, new fare gates in more than 150 stations, modern signals along at least 75 miles.
payload.subject: 2025-2029 Capital Program

payload.claim_text: 272 total subway stations: 154 accessible today, 34 active construction, 79 in development, 5 for Board vote today.
Track progress with the new Capital Dashboard at capitaldashboard.mta.info

In 2025, we implemented 63 initiatives to achieve these savings. Examples: Decreased days lost due Workers' Comp claims by 10% since 2024. Reduced time to hire NYCT from 166 days in 2023 to 105 days in 2025. Installed new OMNY Card Vending Machines which go out-of-service 70% less frequently. Reduced cash collection efforts - collect 68% less cash than in 2018. Processing orders for goods under $1M from 9 days to 7 days.
MTA is more transparent today with 227 data assets published to NYS Open Data Portal.

payload.description: The largest in MTA history. Invests in MTA's $1.5 trillion asset base, includes over 90% of state-of-good-repair investments. Highlights: 2,000 new railcars, 150 subway and 25 railroad station rehabs, new fare gates in more than 150 stations, modern signals along at least 75 miles.
Update to the MTA Board presenting 2025 year in data

evidence_refs.source_quote: Update to the MTA Board December 17, 2025

tool_args.label: LIRR CityTicket annualized 2025 - component of total
Crime down 5.2% year to date
tool_args.raw_text: Crime is down 5.2% year to date

payload.claim_text: ~97% of full fare and 93% of reduced fare customers use Tap and Ride. >300,000 customers reach the fare cap in a typical week. 7-Day MetroCard trip count has fallen ~87% since 2023.
By the end of the 25-29 Plan, no rider will be more than 1 station away from an accessible station. ~70% of all subway rides will take place to or from an accessible station by the end of the plan.
More activity: 16 million more leisure and work visits. Source: NYC EDC, 2025.
